_meta.note: Jojen Paste cluster: mint jojen-paste (concept.theory). Source: ASX video EhtbVpc8E70 ('Jojen Paste: does Bran eat Jojen?'). All quotes byte-verified against sources/chapters/*.md at cited lines by this session (independent grep re-check on top of the regrounding/regrounding-agent substrate). One source-grounding false positive caught and excluded (affc-cersei-10.md:173 is Kevan Lannister speaking, not the Sansa POV line the video's paraphrase implied) — documented in the node's Ungrounded material, no edge minted from it. S216 adjudication: J5 retiered tier-5 (ambiguous pronoun); J8 absence-vs-death scoping note added; all others confirmed as proposed (8C/1A/0R edges, prose CLEAR).

edges.quote: The red veins were only weirwood sap, he supposed, but in the torchlight they looked remarkably like blood.
edges.note: The paste's core physical description — the theory's anchor image. Quote deliberately includes the text's own naturalistic hedge ('he supposed') rather than truncating it out.

edges.quote: My task was to get you here. My part in this is done.
edges.note: Jojen's own words, in the same chapter as the paste-eating, framing his role as complete — read by the theory as a farewell.

edges.quote: He will not even try and fight his fate. He says the greendreams do not lie.
edges.note: Independent corroboration (Meera, not Jojen himself) that Jojen has foreseen and resigned himself to an unstated fate via his prophetic greendreams.

edges.quote: Jojen Reed grew ever more sullen and solitary, to his sister’s distress.
edges.note: Behavioral/mood evidence rather than a direct statement — weaker than J2/J3, tiered down accordingly.

edges.quote: Except he dreamed me dead, and I’m not. Only he was, in a way.
edges.note: Earlier-book foreshadowing the theory reads as pointing at Jojen's eventual death. Tier-4: the second 'he' is genuinely ambiguous in the prose (could refer to Jojen or to a metaphorical death of Bran's former self) and the text does not disambiguate. S216 fresh-verify adjudication: tier-4->tier-5 — the pronoun is genuinely ambiguous and sits two books before the key scene; kept only as the weakest-grade foreshadowing marker.

edges.quote: “Your blood makes you a greenseer,” said Lord Brynden. “This will help awaken your gifts and wed you to the trees.”
edges.note: The text's own stated explanation for the paste's function attributes 'blood' to Bran's own bloodline/gift, not to an added ingredient — complicates (without disproving) the specific inference that the red veins are Jojen's blood. Brynden is established elsewhere as withholding information, so this is not treated as dispositive.

edges.quote: but as his life flowed out of him in a red tide, Brandon Stark could taste the blood.
edges.note: Bran's final vision in the SAME chapter as the paste-eating: an ancient human sacrifice to a heart tree, with Bran tasting real blood through the weirwood roots. Strong same-chapter juxtaposition; the vision itself depicts a different sacrifice/captive/era, not Jojen's death on-page, so the Jojen-specific mapping remains the theory's inference.

edges.quote: He had hoped that Meera and Jojen would be there, so he could tell them what he had seen, but their snug alcove in the rock was cold and empty.
edges.note: Jojen (and Meera) are conspicuously absent immediately after Bran eats the paste; this chapter is the last on-page appearance of Bran (and of Jojen/Meera) in the published books — the theory's circumstantial-timing anchor. S216 adjudication note: the text confirms ABSENCE only, not death — the absence-equals-death step is the theory's inference, not the passage's.

edges.quote: Afterward the gods transformed the cook into a monstrous white rat who could only eat his own young. He had roamed the Nightfort ever since, devouring his children, but still his hunger was not sated.
edges.note: A specific in-world precedent Bran himself recalls (at the Nightfort, earlier in the journey) for unknowing/betrayed cannibalism and its curse — thematic precedent within Bran's own arc, not evidence about the paste itself; tiered down accordingly.
